comment_text.iti86:2.1: This phrase, commonly used in the Suttas, has a precedent in Rig Veda 3.17.1, where the sacred fire is kindled according to the precedent ordained by the first sacrificer.

root_text.iti86:2.2: dhammānudhammapaṭipannoyanti bhāsamāno dhammaññeva bhāsati no adhammaṁ, vitakkayamāno vā dhammavitakkaññeva vitakketi no adhammavitakkaṁ, tadubhayaṁ vā pana abhinivejjetvā upekkhako viharati sato sampajāno”ti.
root_text.iti86:5.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti86:0.4: Practicing In Line With the Teaching
translation_text.iti86:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti86:2.1: “Regarding a mendicant practicing in line with the teaching, it is in line with the teaching to declare that
translation_text.iti86:2.2: this is what it means to practice in line with the teaching. When speaking, they speak on the teaching, not against it. When thinking, they think on the teaching, not against it. And rejecting both, they meditate staying equanimous, mindful and aware.”
translation_text.iti86:3.1: “Delighting in the teaching, enjoying the teaching,
translation_text.iti86:3.3: a mendicant who recollects the teaching
translation_text.iti86:3.4: doesn’t fall away from the true teaching.
translation_text.iti86:5.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
